functions: Receptor tyrosine kinase activity
Phosphorylation-mediated signal transduction upon ligand binding
Promotes tumor cell proliferation, migration, and invasion
Drives pro-proliferative signaling in pancreatic cancer cells
pathways: Toll-like receptor-reactive oxygen species-EGFR signaling axis

disease_associations.disease: Lung adenocarcinoma with uncommon EGFR G719A mutation

pubmed.druggability_notes: EGFR is an established druggable tyrosine kinase target. Inhibited by quinazolinone derivatives and EGFR-tyrosine kinase inhibitors (e.g., afatinib). Amivantamab (anti-EGFR/MET bispecific antibody) plus chemotherapy effective in EGFR-mutant NSCLC including uncommon G719A mutation. In silico molecular docking performed against EGFR (PDB 1XKK) for plant-derived compounds (aspidinol, thunbergol) with binding affinities ~-8.6 kcal/mol. Pharmacologic EGFR inhibition suppresses microbiota-induced proliferative effects.

functions: Receptor tyrosine kinase binding EGF-family ligands (EGF, TGFA, AREG, EPGN, BTC, EREG, HBEGF)
Ligand binding triggers receptor homo- and heterodimerization and autophosphorylation on cytoplasmic residues
Recruits adapter protein GRB2 to activate downstream signaling cascades
Directly phosphorylates RGS16, activating its GTPase activity and coupling to GPCR signaling
Phosphorylates MUC1, increasing its interaction with SRC and CTNNB1/beta-catenin
Positively regulates cell migration via interaction with CCDC88A/GIV
Enhances learning and memory performance
Role in mammalian pain signaling (long-lasting hypersensitivity)
pathways: Epidermal growth factor receptor signaling pathway
Positive regulation of canonical Wnt signaling pathway
uniprot.druggability_notes: EGFR is an ATP-binding tyrosine-protein kinase (ATP-binding, nucleotide-binding keywords) with a well-characterized kinase domain. It is a validated proto-oncogene and therapeutic target in oncology; disease variant keyword and 3D-structure availability support druggability. ATP-binding pocket relevant for tyrosine kinase inhibitor classes.

functions: Receptor tyrosine kinase binding EGF-family ligands (EGF, TGFA, AREG, EPGN, BTC, EREG, HBEGF) to transduce extracellular cues into cellular responses
Ligand binding triggers receptor homo- and heterodimerization and autophosphorylation of cytoplasmic residues
Recruits adapter protein GRB2 to activate downstream signaling cascades
Activates RAS-RAF-MEK-ERK, PI3K-AKT, PLCgamma-PKC and STAT signaling modules; may activate NF-kappa-B
Phosphorylates RGS16 to activate its GTPase activity, coupling EGFR to GPCR signaling
Phosphorylates MUC1, increasing its interaction with SRC and CTNNB1/beta-catenin
Positively regulates cell migration via interaction with CCDC88A/GIV retaining EGFR at the cell membrane
Acts as a receptor for hepatitis C virus entry by promoting CD81-CLDN1 complex formation and membrane fusion
pathways: Constitutive Signaling by Ligand-Responsive EGFR Cancer Variants (R-HSA-1236382)

disease_associations.disease: head and neck squamous cell carcinoma

opentargets.druggability_notes: EGFR is a well-established therapeutic target in NSCLC and other carcinomas; its cytoplasmic tyrosine kinase domain is the canonical druggable site. Oncogenic variants including EGFRvIII and ligand-responsive cancer variants drive constitutive signaling. Text does not detail specific drug classes or binding pockets, but kinase-domain autophosphorylation activity provides a clear small-molecule target rationale.
